questions.question: Research suggests what percentage of entrepreneurs experience imposter syndrome?
questions.explanation: 84% of entrepreneurs report feelings of imposter syndrome, which directly impacts their pricing decisions.

questions.question: Psychologically, why do founders underprice their products?

options.text: As a shield against the risk of rejection

questions.explanation: Low prices lower the stakes. If someone rejects a cheap product, it doesn't feel like a judgment of your worth. A high price invites judgment.

questions.question: What is a negative consequence of setting your price too low in B2B?

options.text: You signal a lack of credibility or enterprise-readiness
You make too much money too fast

questions.explanation: In B2B, price is often a proxy for quality. A price that is too low can signal that your solution is a 'toy' rather than a serious business tool.

questions.question: Who are generally the most demanding customers?

options.text: Price-sensitive buyers paying the lowest tier

questions.explanation: Buyers who value money over time (price sensitive) tend to have higher expectations for support and attention relative to what they pay.

questions.question: What happened in the 'Fractional Consulting' case study when Mike raised his prices 5x?

options.text: He lost all his clients and went broke
He kept all his clients and made 5x more
He lost 80% of clients but made the same revenue with 20% of the work

questions.explanation: Raising prices is a filter. It removed the low-value clients and allowed him to earn the same revenue with significantly less workload.
